active_calculations: Phase 14 P14-0 through P14-4 passed without training; P14-5 objective repair, P14-6 short proof, and P14-7 segmented long run remain ordered gates.
No training may launch until P14-5 is complete and P14-6 is explicitly scoped.
P13-H failed; P13-I exact claims are authoritative; P13-F HTP ReLU is execution-only; residual-adapter OpenCL is not a fused megakernel; train-loss movement cannot replace full-heldout movement.
blockers: Phase 10 six-hour endurance is promoted only for the current rank-4 two-layer phone-native lane. Full Gemma4 training, Hexagon NPU training, public benchmark readiness, and theoretical maximum remain blocked.

H11-E did not promote expanded adapter ranks or projection LoRA. Rank-16 and rank-32 were finite and mutable but showed zero two-iteration loss reduction; projection LoRA remains blocked until layer-internal backward kernels and checkpoint layout exist.

H11-F top-k KL objective pass is narrow: deterministic held-out movement is positive but tiny, so it does not support public benchmark readiness, broad capability, full Gemma4 training, or stronger scope claims.

GPD phase-add succeeded for Phase 11, but generic GPD state synchronization rewrote .gpd/state.json in a lossy parser format during this PRD-writing task. The machine state was restored and updated via structured JSON; future agents should parse-check .gpd/state.json after GPD CLI state commands.

Phase 13 forbids non-Gemma artifacts inside promoted Gemma gates. Any Qwen, SmolLM, random-init, hidden-size mismatch, or artifact not bridged into the Gemma runtime is an immediate failure and can only be retained as a negative tool-surface probe.
Phase 13 requires real corpus scale before learning promotion. A 16-sequence cache is a smoke test only, not a corpus-scale learning gate.
P13-H failed after 1742/5000 updates under thermal safety; full-heldout baseline/trained evals did not pass and no heldout KL improvement is promoted.

RunPod /workspace/Polymath-AI remains quarantined as stale for Phase 14 authority/oracle work; use clean detached worktree /workspace/Polymath-AI-phase14-gemma4 for gate-specific offline oracle commands.

P13-G raw/bin HTP ReLU payloads and local build caches were moved outside the repo to /Users/Zer0pa/Polymat AI/.artifact_quarantine/Polymath-AI/20260525T164328Z_phase14_p14_0; compact manifest repair remains P14-2.
blockers_concerns: H11-G does not promote mutable QAIRT sections or HTP zero-order training: the active qwen_block QNN context reports zero updateable tensors, and RunPod x86 LoRA tooling still has onnx/pydantic/libc++.so.1 environment gaps. HTP is frozen-forward/teacher only for H11-H.

decisions.rationale: Prevents documentation-as-progress and preserves the authority metric.
decisions.summary: Phase 11 documentation is only a control artifact; execution progress begins only when H11 gate artifacts are produced by phone experiments.

decisions.rationale: Prevents Phase 12 Qwen/random-init hidden-size mismatch from contaminating Gemma heterogeneous claims.
decisions.summary: Promoted Gemma gates must be Gemma4-only; non-Gemma HTP artifacts are negative probes only.
Phase 14 selected by P13-I: repair scaled heldout learning before new hardware claims.
decisions.rationale: P13-H failed the authority heldout gate under thermal safety, so cleanup, heldout evaluator repair, and objective repair precede any new long phone-native run.

acceptance_tests.pass_condition: p50 cosine >= 0.99, failed non-pad tokens below threshold = 0, claimed backend is phone OpenCL/Vulkan, and no artifact policy violation occurs.
acceptance_tests.procedure: Run or preserve the G1 E4B layer 0 OpenCL phone regression with real pretrained weights, non-pad token cosine comparison, and deterministic repeat evidence after material runtime changes.
acceptance_tests.subject: claim-g1-regression-floor

acceptance_tests.pass_condition: All 13 terminal acceptance conditions in the authority PRD are satisfied, including G1 remaining green and a sustained authority run or predeclared objective completion.
acceptance_tests.procedure: Execute the PRD terminal gate: HF raw text streams directly to phone, tokenization and packing run on phone, phone runtime performs forward/backward/update, frozen hashes stay stable, trainable tensors mutate, and a replayable checkpoint or adapter is emitted.
acceptance_tests.subject: claim-terminal-phone-training

acceptance_tests.pass_condition: All critical falsifiers pass or are explicitly resolved; no unresolved critical falsifier remains.
acceptance_tests.procedure: Run the G10 falsifier checklist against the promoted gate claim and rerun or fix any unresolved critical issue.
acceptance_tests.subject: claim-terminal-phone-training

allowed_estimator_families: FP64 cosine comparisons against RunPod PyTorch references for forward and gradient fixtures
Hash-stability checks for frozen tensors and mutation checks for declared trainable tensors
Replayable checkpoint or adapter validation manifests
allowed_fit_families: Evidence-driven trainable-scope expansion from adapter or low-rank deltas
Backend choice by equal-correctness phone measurement
forbidden_estimator_families: Throughput-only, compile-only, local test-only, or report-only proxies
CPU fallback masquerading as Adreno/OpenCL/Vulkan authority execution
forbidden_fit_families: Broad corpus construction before the first phone-native training slice works
formulations: Gate-ladder execution from G0 through G10 with G1 as non-regressing floor
Model-specific Gemma 4 static or megakernel runtime with explicit tensor contracts and replayable manifests
stop_and_rethink_conditions: G1 p50 cosine falls below 0.99 or failed non-pad tokens become nonzero after material runtime changes
The training runtime depends on Mac or RunPod for tokenization, packing, minibatch serving, gradients, or optimizer updates
Frozen base tensor hashes mutate unexpectedly
A command risks destructive phone changes without rollback
License ambiguity blocks the selected model or corpus artifact

claims.statement: Polymath-AI can execute a real Gemma 4 native training run on REDMAGIC SM8750 from HF raw text stream through phone checkpoint or adapter emission without host runtime data-path substitution.
The passed Gemma 4 E4B layer 0 OpenCL phone gate is a permanent regression floor and not a terminal training claim.

context_gaps: Formal GPD project files were missing before this repair; fallback hypotheses and runlog existed only
Gemma4 Kernel import into Polymath is not yet complete until source, tests, runbooks, and manifest hashes are committed
known_good_baselines: Upstream commit c5b6e3522d28d0e1dc56084cb97fa9e95e29aa4e for the passed phone OpenCL gate
user_asserted_anchors: G1 passed: google/gemma-4-E4B layer 0 OpenCL on REDMAGIC NX789J / SM8750 with p50 cosine 0.9999890020383452, min cosine 0.9999801999737985, failed tokens 0, repeat output byte-identical
Phone is the authority runtime; RunPod is build/reference oracle only; Mac is control plane only

deliverables.description: Imported source, runbooks, and comparator path that preserve and rerun the G1 phone OpenCL regression without forbidden artifact leakage.
must_contain: no model weights or raw binaries in git

deliverables.description: Phone-native Gemma 4 runtime including tokenizer, sequence packer, backend executor, backward path, optimizer update, telemetry, and checkpoint store.
Checkpoint or adapter manifest with hashes, replay data, frozen/trainable tensor hash checks, and loss/gradient telemetry.
Adversarial review of model revision, weight provenance, backend execution, host-path absence, tolerances, checkpoint replay, and G1 preservation.

forbidden_proxies.proxy: Mac build, CTest, RunPod compile, or local tests presented as authority training progress without phone gate evidence.
forbidden_proxies.reason: Local green is not truth; the phone runtime is the authority metric.
forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-terminal-phone-training

forbidden_proxies.proxy: A small demo or report-only pipeline treated as a training appliance.
forbidden_proxies.reason: The terminal gate requires real phone-native streaming, tokenization, packing, training update, checkpoint, G1 regression, and falsifier survival.
forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-terminal-phone-training

forbidden_proxies.proxy: The passed layer 0 forward gate inflated into a fused megakernel, backward, optimizer, training, NPU, or sustained thermal claim.
forbidden_proxies.reason: G1 is a regression floor only; it does not satisfy later gates.
forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-g1-regression-floor

forbidden_proxies.proxy: Mac or RunPod secretly serves tokenization, sequence packing, minibatches, gradients, or optimizer state during the authority run.
forbidden_proxies.reason: That would violate the PRD runtime topology and invalidate the terminal claim.
forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-terminal-phone-training

observables.definition: FP64 per-token cosine against RunPod PyTorch reference for non-pad tokens on REDMAGIC Adreno OpenCL
observables.name: G1 E4B layer 0 OpenCL phone parity

observables.definition: Validated checkpoint or adapter emitted by a REDMAGIC-native HF stream, tokenization, UFS packing, forward, backward, and optimizer loop
observables.name: Phone-native Gemma 4 training artifact

observables.definition: Pre/post hashes for frozen Gemma base tensors remain unchanged while declared trainable tensors mutate
observables.name: Frozen base tensor hash stability

aliases: Gemma 4 Snapdragon Megakernel Native Phone Training PRD
references.why_it_matters: Defines the terminal objective, gate ladder, artifact policy, GPD requirements, and true blockers.
Prevents proxy wins, local-green narratives, soft refusal by implementation, and premature completion claims.
Provides the regression floor metrics and artifact pointers for the first authority phone gate.
Documents commands, evidence locations, implementation notes, tests, and non-claims for the passed phone gate.
Records inspection entry points and non-claims after the authority gate pass.
Defines the only approved landing zone and artifact exclusions for importing Gemma4 Kernel into Polymath.

in_scope: Gemma 4 model-specific phone runtime work on REDMAGIC NX789J / SM8750
OpenCL/Vulkan Adreno forward, backward, reduction, and optimizer kernels
Phone CPU tokenization, UFS packed sequence cache, training telemetry, checkpoint or adapter emission
RunPod as build server and PyTorch oracle only
G1 E4B layer 0 OpenCL gate preservation as a regression floor
out_of_scope: Qwen frozen-middle correctness as an active route
Mac or RunPod tokenization, packing, minibatch serving, gradient computation, or optimizer update in the authority runtime path
Public benchmark, release, or deployment claims before license and falsifier gates close
scope.question: Can Polymath-AI execute a real Gemma 4 native training run on REDMAGIC SM8750, with phone-native Hugging Face text streaming, CPU tokenization, UFS sequence packing, Adreno/Hexagon/CPU training execution, and a validated checkpoint or adapter artifact?
unresolved_questions: Which minimum adapter or low-rank trainable scope fits the first phone backward/update gate without violating frozen base hashes?
Which license-clean Hugging Face text slice is acceptable for the first authority training batch?
Whether OpenCL remains the first training backend or Vulkan earns the route after equal correctness evidence

competing_explanations: G1 may reflect a correct one-layer forward implementation while the training memory/backward path still exceeds phone constraints
A phone data pipeline can pass tokenizer/packing parity while still failing integrated training due backend or checkpoint constraints
disconfirming_observations: G1 regression fails after import or refactor
A supposed authority training run requires host-side tokenization, packing, gradients, optimizer state, or minibatch serving
Frozen base hashes change during an adapter-scope update
unvalidated_assumptions: OpenCL source import can be built in the Polymath namespace without semantic drift from upstream
Existing phone runner artifacts remain sufficient to rerun G1 until the imported build replaces them
weakest_anchors: First trainable-scope memory and correctness budget is not yet proven on phone
HF corpus/license choice for the first streamed batch is not yet locked

project_reference.core_research_question: Can Polymath-AI execute and validate a real Gemma 4 training run natively on REDMAGIC SM8750 without substituting Mac or RunPod for any runtime data-path stage?
project_reference.current_focus: Phase 14: repair scaled heldout learning before new hardware claims

session.stopped_at: Phase 14 P14-4 full-heldout evaluator repair; no training launched.
